mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength and robust corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and impending earnings revisions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of contraction periods, or the mechanism fails to predict direction during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation rate exceeding 3.0% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of high-inflation months, or the signal shows no predictive power when inflation exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumer demand, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of payroll growth periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining housing starts and falling retail sales indicates a synchronized demand shock across durable and nondurable goods, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Synchronized declines yield positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances, or the joint signal loses predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production growth above 1.0% month-over-month during early recovery phases signals improving capacity utilization and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of acceleration periods, or the mechanism fails to predict returns outside early recovery regimes.
